assumptions: No JFrog account, subscription, platform deployment, hostname, project, local repository, permission assignment, access token, artifact file, or API client configuration is assumed.
A browser, working email address or GitHub identity, network connection, and a small local file are available.
No account submission, email verification, service provisioning, login, repository creation, token generation, or artifact deployment was performed during research.

developer_goal: Start without a JFrog account, open the official cloud trial, provision a JFrog Platform deployment, create a Generic local repository, create an access token, upload a small file with the Artifactory REST API, and receive HTTP 201 Created.

first_success.outcome: A developer-controlled file is stored at a chosen path in a newly created Generic local Artifactory repository.
first_success.observable_signal: The PUT request to `/artifactory/{repoKey}/{filePath}` returns HTTP 201 Created, which the API reference defines as successful artifact deployment.

route_selection.surface: JFrog Artifactory Cloud first Generic local repository artifact deployment
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: JFrog Artifactory Cloud first Generic local repository artifact deployment
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: JFrog Platform Tour
candidates.reason: The tour is read-only and uses sample data, so it does not prove that the developer can store an artifact in their own service.

candidates.name: Artifactory web UI deployment
candidates.reason: The UI upload flow is documented and accessible after repository creation, but the REST deployment endpoint supplies a clearer observable success response.

candidates.name: Self-hosted Artifactory trial
candidates.reason: Self-hosting adds software download, infrastructure, installation, and service operation before the same repository outcome.

required_path.action: Complete JFrog account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new JFrog account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected JFrog candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The PUT request to `/artifactory/{repoKey}/{filePath}` returns HTTP 201 Created, which the API reference defines as successful artifact deployment.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Create or use a JFrog identity and obtain access to a provisioned JFrog Platform deployment through the 14-day cloud trial.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'legal', 'requirement': 'Accept the trial terms and JFrog privacy processing presented by the cloud-trial form.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'region', 'requirement': 'Choose the cloud provider and region offered by the trial form and provide an available hostname for the deployment.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

external_gates.description: The developer must create a JFrog identity through GitHub or email before a writable cloud deployment is available.
The public form does not expose the complete verification and credential sequence after the initial identity submission.
The developer must choose a deployment hostname, cloud provider, and region.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Deploy an artifact to a local Artifactory repository with the Deploy Artifact REST API.

excluded.item: Artifact download and verification, package-manager setup, remote and virtual repositories, build-info publication, CI integration, Xray scanning, policies, promotion, release bundles, distribution, r
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Start a Trial With Artifactory and Xray | JFrog
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does JFrog show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
